title: Engineering Mathematics - Linear Algebra - Trace of Matrix

questions.question:
Consider the collection \( \mathcal{C} \) of all possible \( n \times n \) matrices with entries in \( \{1, \ldots, n\} \) that obey the following property:

  •  every row of \( M \) is a permutation of \( \{1,2, \ldots, n\} \) and every column of \( M \) is a permutation of \( \{1,2, \ldots, n\} \).

An example of such a matrix for \( n=3 \) is the following.

\[
M=\left[\begin{array}{lll}
3 & 2 & 1 \\
1 & 3 & 2 \\
2 & 1 & 3
\end{array}\right]
\]
Suppose we choose a matrix \( X \) from \( \mathcal{C} \) uniformly at random. What is the expected value of \( \textsf{trace}(X)? \) Recall that the trace of a matrix is the sum of its diagonal entries.



Linear Algebra: TIFR CSE 2025 | Part A | Question: 8
